name: Ascend Transcript Parser
description: Parses meeting transcript files (.txt, .md) into structured data with speaker turns. Provides pure parsing functions for extracting speaker turns and dates, plus database-aware helpers for member resolution and duplicate detection. Handles filename-based and content-based metadata extraction.

types.name: TranscriptError

fields.name: variant
fields.description: Error category: FILE_NOT_FOUND, FILE_UNREADABLE, EMPTY_TRANSCRIPT, INVALID_FORMAT, or DIRECTORY_NOT_FOUND

types.description: Custom exception for transcript parsing failures. Extends ValueError with variant categorization.

types.name: TranscriptTurn

fields.name: speaker
fields.description: Speaker name extracted from transcript line

fields.name: text
fields.description: Text content spoken by the speaker, may span multiple lines

types.description: A single speaker turn in a transcript. Immutable (frozen=True in Pydantic model_config).

types.name: ParsedTranscript

fields.name: raw_text
fields.description: Full transcript text (excluded from serialization via Field(exclude=True))

fields.name: date
fields.description: Meeting date in YYYY-MM-DD format if detected, None otherwise

fields.name: member_name
fields.description: Member name extracted from filename if present, None otherwise

fields.name: member_id
fields.description: Database member ID, initially None after parsing

types.description: Structured representation of a parsed meeting transcript with extracted metadata.

functions.name: parse_transcript
functions.description: Read a transcript file from disk and parse it into a structured ParsedTranscript object. Extracts speaker turns using regex pattern matching, detects date from filename/content, and extracts member name from filename. Truncates files exceeding MAX_RAW_TEXT (100,000 chars).

inputs.name: file_path
inputs.description: Path to the transcript file (.txt or .md)

error_cases.name: not_a_file
error_cases.condition: file_path exists but is not a regular file

error_cases.name: file_unreadable
error_cases.condition: PermissionError or OSError when reading file

error_cases.name: empty_transcript
error_cases.condition: File content is empty or only whitespace

preconditions: file_path must be a regular file (not directory)
file must be readable with UTF-8 encoding
file must not be empty (after stripping whitespace)
postconditions: Returns ParsedTranscript with source_file set to filename
raw_text contains file content (truncated to MAX_RAW_TEXT if needed)
turns list contains parsed speaker turns (may be empty if no matches)
date is extracted if found in filename or first 20 lines of content
member_name is extracted from filename pattern YYYY-MM-DD_Name if present
member_id is always None (requires separate resolution)
side_effects: Logs warning if file exceeds MAX_RAW_TEXT

structured_side_effects.description: Reads transcript file with UTF-8 encoding
Warns if transcript exceeds MAX_RAW_TEXT characters

functions.name: detect_date
functions.description: Extract a meeting date in YYYY-MM-DD format from filename (priority) or content (first 20 lines). Validates extracted dates using datetime.date.fromisoformat.

inputs.name: filename
inputs.description: Filename to search for date pattern

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: Transcript content to search if filename has no date

postconditions: Returns YYYY-MM-DD string if valid date found in filename (checked first)
Returns YYYY-MM-DD string if valid date found in first 20 lines of content (checked second)
Returns None if no valid date found in either location

functions.name: resolve_member
functions.description: Match speaker names from transcript against members database to identify the meeting subject (team member). Uses exact matching (name, email prefix, slack handle) followed by fuzzy matching (SequenceMatcher ratio >= 0.6). Excludes manager_name from matching.

inputs.name: speaker_names
inputs.description: List of speaker names from transcript

inputs.name: manager_name
inputs.description: Manager name to exclude from matching, None if not filtering

inputs.name: db_conn
inputs.description: SQLite database connection with members table

preconditions: db_conn must be a valid, open SQLite connection
Database must have 'members' table with columns: id, name, email, slack
postconditions: Returns member id (int) if exact or fuzzy match found (excluding manager)
Returns None if no match found
Exact matches (name, email prefix, slack) are tried before fuzzy matching
Fuzzy matching uses FUZZY_THRESHOLD=0.6 on lowercased names
side_effects: Reads from members table in database

structured_side_effects.description: Executes SELECT query on members table

functions.name: scan_directory
functions.description: Find all transcript files (.txt, .md extensions) in a directory. Non-recursive scan that filters hidden files, non-files, and zero-byte files. Returns sorted list by filename.

inputs.name: dir_path
inputs.description: Directory path to scan for transcript files

error_cases.name: not_a_directory
error_cases.condition: dir_path exists but is not a directory

postconditions: Returns list of Path objects for .txt and .md files
List is sorted alphabetically by filename
Hidden files (starting with '.') are excluded
Non-file entries (directories, symlinks) are excluded
Zero-byte files are excluded
Case-insensitive extension matching (.txt, .TXT, .md, .MD)
side_effects: Reads directory metadata and file stats

structured_side_effects.description: Iterates directory entries and reads file metadata

functions.name: check_duplicate
functions.description: Check if a transcript with matching source_file, member_id, and date already exists in the meetings table. Uses IS operator for NULL-safe comparison of optional fields.

inputs.name: member_id
inputs.description: Member ID to check, None for no member

inputs.name: date
inputs.description: Meeting date to check, None for no date

inputs.name: db_conn
inputs.description: SQLite database connection with meetings table

preconditions: db_conn must be a valid, open SQLite connection
Database must have 'meetings' table with columns: source_file, member_id, date
postconditions: Returns True if at least one matching row exists in meetings table
Returns False if no matching rows exist
side_effects: Reads from meetings table in database

structured_side_effects.description: Executes SELECT COUNT query on meetings table

functions.name: _validate_date
functions.description: Private helper function to validate a date string using datetime.date.fromisoformat. Returns True if valid ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD), False otherwise.
postconditions: Returns True if candidate is valid ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
Returns False if candidate is invalid or cannot be parsed

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Constructor for TranscriptError exception. Initializes the ValueError base class with message and stores the variant string.
postconditions: Sets self.variant to provided variant string
Calls super().__init__(message) to initialize ValueError

structured_side_effects.description: Sets variant attribute on exception instance

invariants: SPEAKER_PATTERN matches lines like 'Name: text' or '**Name**: text' with speaker names 2-60 chars
CONTENT_DATE_PATTERN matches YYYY-MM-DD anywhere in text
MAX_RAW_TEXT = 100,000 characters (files are truncated at this limit)
Date strings are always in YYYY-MM-DD ISO format when present
Only .txt and .md file extensions are recognized as transcripts
Speaker turns accumulate continuation lines until next speaker pattern match
Database queries use IS operator for NULL-safe comparison of optional fields

data_access.rationale: Module reads transcript files from filesystem and queries members/meetings tables for resolution and duplicate checking. No writes to database or filesystem.

side_effects.rationale: parse_transcript reads file content, scan_directory reads directory metadata
resolve_member queries members table, check_duplicate queries meetings table
parse_transcript logs warning when truncating oversized files

authority.rationale: This module owns the parsing logic for Ascend meeting transcript files, including speaker turn extraction, date detection, and filename-based metadata extraction. It is the authoritative parser for the transcript format.
